wiki.summary: "Without a Face" is a song by American rap metal band Rage Against the Machine from their second studio album Evil Empire (1996). "Without a Face" discusses the hardships faced by Mexican immigrants when moving to the United States, and how the U.S. government was building a wall at the border between itself and Mexico to stop the immigrants coming in. The title references the fact that many Mexican immigrants in the U.S. go unnoticed: they don't have signs on their backs Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Without a Face" is a song by American rap metal band Rage Against the Machine from their second studio album Evil Empire (1996). "Without a Face" discusses the hardships faced by Mexican immigrants when moving to the United States, and how the U.S. government was building a wall at the border between itself and Mexico to stop the immigrants coming in. The title references the fact that many Mexican immigrants in the U.S. go unnoticed: they don't have signs on their backs, identification or rights, and are treated as one mass of nameless people. As such, they can be thought of as "without a face". Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.
